diagnosed: Bot-dispatched research escalations are broken. A scout/coverage-critic escalation dispatches research-agent.yml via `gh workflow run` from inside a job (scripts/escalate-research.js), so the triggering actor is github-actions[bot]. claude-code-action@v1 now refuses bot-initiated runs unless the bot is allow-listed: run 29722743035 (workflow_dispatch, deep tier) died with 'Action failed with error: Workflow initiated by non-human actor: github-actions (type: Bot). Add bot to allowed_bots list or use (star) to allow all bots.' The Fable deep step swallows it (continue-on-error:true -> reads as 'no commit' -> fallback), then the Opus deep-fallback step (no continue-on-error) fails the whole job. Every future escalation will fail identically. Scheduled standard runs are unaffected (human actor, green).
notes: ROOT-CAUSE FIX LIVES IN A PROTECTED PATH the mechanic cannot author. The only correct fix is in .github/workflows/research-agent.yml; the protect-automation.js PreToolUse hook (CI-only) blocked the edit ('A human must author changes to these files'). No non-protected-path alternative exists: allowed_bots is a claude-code-action input, and the bot actor is inherent to `gh workflow run` issued from CI. Abandoned rather than open a PR I cannot author.

FIX FOR A HUMAN: add `allowed_bots: "*"` under `with:` on all three anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 steps in research-agent.yml (Opus standard, Fable deep, Opus deep fallback). `*` is safe here because workflow_dispatch already requires actions:write, so no unprivileged bot can reach it; scope to github-actions[bot] if tighter is preferred. This closes the escalation path.

Triaged as NOT self-repair's job: static-pipeline 2026-07-17 = GitHub Pages 403 (transient infra; pipeline green since). ios-release 2026-07-18 = protected-branch push rejection, already patched 2026-07-19 (rebase-retry loop) and a protected path, not reproducible locally. Older scheduled research failures (2026-07-08..07-16) = transient claude-code-action is_error blips, no longer recurring (last 11 scheduled runs all green).

Health baseline this run: npm test = 753 passing; node scripts/validate-events.js = 57 events, 0 errors; usage-state.json green (23% session, 4% week), so no failure is attributable to the quota governor.
